message:
test(providers): use Drop guard for env-var restoration in #6 tests

Third-pass review caught that the prior test code did manual env-var
restoration at the end of each test body. If an assertion failed
earlier in the test, the restore was unreachable and the env var
leaked into subsequent tests (or, across runs, into the user's
shell if the test binary was run directly and panicked).

Replaced the manual pattern with an `EnvVarGuard` Drop guard per
test module. The guard stashes the previous value at construction
and restores it in Drop, so cleanup runs unconditionally on normal
return, assertion panic, or any other unwind.

Drop order — Rust destroys locals in reverse declaration order, so
the env var is restored (_env drops) before the mutex is released
(_lock drops). This prevents a window where the mutex is free but
the env var still holds the test value.

No behavior change on the happy path; purely defensive against
panicking tests poisoning later tests in the same process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
